content.problemStatement:
Write a Python program to demonstrate Exception Handling Techniques.


Create a user-defined exception called `Validate`.
Raise this exception with the message: `"Person is not eligible to vote"`

if the age is less than 18.

Input Format
A single integer representing the person's age.



Output Format

If the person is eligible (age ≥ 18), print `"Eligible to vote"`.
If not, raise and handle a custom exception with the message `"Person is not eligible to vote"`.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 100). Each of the next T lines contains a single integer age (0 ≤ age ≤ 150).
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output a single line:
- If age ≥ 18, print "Eligible to vote".
- Otherwise, print "Person is not eligible to vote".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Age 20 is ≥ 18, so the person is eligible.

sampleTestCases.output: Person is not eligible to vote
sampleTestCases.explanation: Age 16 is < 18, the custom exception is raised and its message is printed.

sampleTestCases.output:
Eligible to vote
Person is not eligible to vote
Eligible to vote
sampleTestCases.explanation: First age 18 → eligible. Second age 17 → ineligible. Third age 25 → eligible.

hidden.label: minimum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: Person is not eligible to vote

hidden.label: edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Person is not eligible to vote
Eligible to vote

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
Eligible to vote
Person is not eligible to vote
Eligible to vote

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Eligible to vote
Eligible to vote
Person is not eligible to vote
Eligible to vote

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times reading an age. For each age, if it is less than 18, raise a user‑defined exception (Validate) with the required message and catch it to print the message. If the age is 18 or more, simply print the success message. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used besides the output buffer.
